standards.statement: The practice's clinical and managerial leaders are identified and are collectively responsible for defining the practice's mission and creating the plans and policies needed to fulfil the mission.

criteria.description: The leaders of the practice are formally or informally identified.
The practice has a mission statement that reflects its strategic objectives and matches the needs of the community served.
The leaders are collectively responsible for ensuring that the mission statement is known to all personnel, patients, carers and the community served.
The practice participates in community health activities.
The leaders, in liaison with the whole practice team, coordinates the compilation of an annual strategic plan and budget.
The leaders are collectively responsible for implementing the practice's mission and strategic plan.
The strategic plan is evaluated on an annual basis.
Regular monitoring of the implementation of the strategic plan against envisaged timeframes and progress in achieving its objectives is documented at intervals determined by the practice.
Where appropriate, the leadership roles in various positions are documented, agreed to and known by the personnel.

standards.statement: A manager is responsible for operating the practice within relevant laws and regulations.

criteria.description: There are documented risk management processes for identifying all risks relating to practice processes and systems, personnel, patients, visitors to the practice and physical facilities.
The manager has the education and/or experience necessary to carry out his or her responsibilities.
The practice is licensed in terms of relevant legislation for the level and type of services provided.
The manager ensures that there is a system in place to monitor the implementation of applicable laws and regulations.
There is evidence of response to any reports from inspecting and regulatory authorities.
The manager implements processes to manage and control human, financial and other resources.

standards.statement: The practice facilitates communication between teams and individuals within the practice.

criteria.description: The practice leaders facilitate communication between teams where relevant and between individual personnel members.
Agendas are prepared for meetings in order to allow those attending to prepare for participation.
Minutes of meetings are taken and are circulated to all relevant personnel.
There is a procedure to make sure that important matters resulting from management meetings are communicated to and acted upon by personnel.

standards.statement: The practice engages with a range of health, community and disability services to plan and facilitate optimal patient care.

criteria.description: Information on services, hours of operation and processes for obtaining care is provided to services in the community who work in collaboration with the practice.
The practice determines that the receiving individual/organisation can meet the patient’s continuing care needs, and establishes arrangements to ensure continuity.
